pattern: Every palindrome grows symmetrically from a center, so expanding outward from each of the 2n-1 centers finds the longest in quadratic time without extra space.
hints: A palindrome is defined by its middle; how many possible middles does a string of length n have?
From each center, push outward while the mirrored characters match - remember both single-character and between-two-character centers.
For every index treat it as an odd center and as the left of an even center, expand while s[l] == s[r], and keep the first widest span seen, returning that substring.

edge_inputs.description: single character is its own palindrome
two equal characters form an even palindrome
no repeats, answer is any single character (the first)
two competing centers of equal length, earliest wins

stress.description: string built around a planted long palindrome over a small alphabet
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    alphabet = 'abc'
    core_len = rng.randrange(1, max(2, n // 2))
    half = [rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(core_len // 2)]
    core = half + ([rng.choice(alphabet)] if core_len % 2 else []) + half[::-1]
    rest = n - len(core)
    left = rest // 2
    right = rest - left
    s = [rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(left)] + core + [rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(right)]
    return [''.join(s[:n])]
stress.note: small alphabet plus a planted palindrome stresses center expansion
